titleStmt.title: SII 28.138: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 138. (A.R. No. 293 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, First prākāra, north wall.

This record and many other records in this location seem to have been recopies just as it is stated specifically in the case of No. 135 above. It is mechanically done with many mistakes. The above fact is also probably indicated by the mention that this record had originally been ordered to be inscribed on the walls of Vikkiramachōḻaṉ-tirumāḷigai, but it is now found in the first prākāra of the temple. It is possible to argue that the first prākāra itself was designated as the above said tirumāḷigai. However, it is seem that it was tiruvedir-ambalam mentioned as the original place where No. 135 had been inscribed which was the place designated as Vikkiramachōḻaṉtirumāḷigai or the Kulōttuṅga-chōḻaṉ-tirumāḷigai. It would also appear that these tirumāḷigai's were dismantled or renovated at a subsequent date requiring the records there on to be inscribed above here to the first prākāra.

The present record is a sequel to No. 133 above. A further plot of land measuring (1/2) vēli and a kāṇi in the same Kaḍavāchchēri alias Tillaināyaka-nallūr, in Gaṅgaikoṇḍachōḻa-ppēriḷamai-nāḍu, was purchased and gifted after getting it freed from taxes (the differences in the names of some of the levies as between No. 133 and the present record might have been the result of clerical errors in one of these) through an order (niyōgam) of mūlaparushaiyar for arranging the supply of flowers to the God and Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam-uḍaiya-periya-nāchchiyār in the temple. Apparently A[ṉumaṉuḻā]n vēḷān Sūriya-dēvaṉ alias Koṅgarāyaṉ of Iḍaiyāṟu in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-nāḍu in Naḍuvil-nāḍu alias Irājarājavaḷanāḍu, who set up the garden for the above purpose as per No. 133 above, was again the donor of the land gifted as per the present record.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī ḥ§ śrīmelkittikkumel koṟcaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu patiṉoṉṟāvatu nāḷ Aimpattāṟiṉāl villavarāyaṉ Olai teṉṉavaṉ brahmārāyarum vikkirama coḻabrahmārāyarum jayatuṅkap pallavaraiyarum kurukulattaraiyarum Uṭaiyār ti
rucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyār koyil śrīmāheśvarakkaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷum śrīkāriyañ ceyvārkaḷum cāmutāyañ ceyvārkaḷum koyil nāyakañ ceyvārkaḷum tirumāḷikaikkūṟu ceyvārkaḷum paṇṭārap pottakamuṭaiyārkaḷum kaṇakkarum kaṇṭu viṭutantāvatu naṭuviThe continuation of the lines is builtin.
l nāṭāṉa Irācarācavaḷanāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭi nāṭṭu Iṭaiyāṟṟu nāṭṭu Iṭaiyāṟṟu Aṉumaṉuḻāṉ veḷāṉ cūriya tevaṉāṉa koṅkarāyaṉ koyilukkum tirukkāmakkoṭṭamuṭaiya periya nācciyār koyilukkum tiruppaḷḷittāmam Ākkic cevikkap perum The continuation of the lines is builtin.
kaikaṅkaikoṇṭa coḻapperiḷamai nāṭṭuk kaṭavācceriyāṉa tillaināyaka nallūrppāl cuntaracoḻavatikku moṟkuRead meṟkuUttama coḻa vāykkālukku teṟku Aiñcāṅ kaṇṇāṟṟu mutaṟcātirattuRead catirattumuṉṉāḷil kūttāṇṭār pakkalum tiruvaiyāṟuṭaiyār piḷḷaikaḷ pakkalum vilaikoṇṭut� The continuation of the lines is builtin.
yirattukkum tiruttoṇṭu ceykiṟa tirumeṉikaḷukku muṉṉāḷil Ikkaṭṭaḷaiyil vilaikoṇṭu Iṟaiyiliyāka kalveṭṭi viṭṭa nilam veli nikki Ittirunantavaṉattuk kiḻakkuppaṭṭa nilamāy vācciyaṉ tiruvattiśvaramuṭaiyāṉ celvappiḷḷaiyāṉ pakkal vilaikoṇṭu Ittirunantavaṉattu The continuation of the lines is builtin.
pāyavum vilaikoṇṭa nilam kāṇiyum Āka nilam Araiye kāṇiyum Iṉṉāḷ mutaliRead mutalmuṉpu Itirunantavaṉattukku viṭṭa nilattuṭaṉe kūṭṭikkaṭamai kuṭimai viṉiyoka maṭaarici tiruvaraṅkāḷapaṇikkottuc cervai nellukkaḷapiṭi veṭṭikkācu maṟṟuḷḷa Aṉaittu vaṟkaṅkaḷun tavirntu Continuation of the lines is builtin.
liyūr mulaparuṣaiyār niyokam Eḻutiṉamaiyil Iṉṉiyokappaṭi Ivvaṉaittu vaṟkaṅkaḷum tavirntu Iṟaiyiliyāka vikkiramacoḻaṉ tirumāḷikaiyile kalveṭṭavum paṇṇuvate Ippaṭi ceyyap paṇṇuvate Ivai villavarāyaṉ Eḻuttu ḥ §
Digital edition of SII 28.138 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
